title: WritableSecretStore::put() for K8sBackend
description:
What: Implement async put() on K8sBackend using create-or-merge-patch semantics, and register with WritableBackendRegistration.

Algorithm:
1. In put(&self, value: SecretValue) -> Result<(), SecretError>:
   a. let client = self.client.get_or_try_init(|| async { build_client().await }).await?.clone()
   b. let api: Api<k8s_openapi::api::core::v1::Secret> = Api::namespaced(client, &self.namespace)
   c. Build the data map:
      - let key_name = self.key.as_deref().unwrap_or("value")
      - let bytes: Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> = value.into_bytes()
      - let data: BTreeMap<String, ByteString> = BTreeMap::from([(key_name.to_owned(), ByteString(bytes.to_vec()))])
      - // bytes stays Zeroizing; ByteString(bytes.to_vec()) clones but that copy is dropped with data after the call
   d. Build the patch Secret object:
      - let patch_secret = k8s_openapi::api::core::v1::Secret { data: Some(data), ..Default::default() }
   e. If self.key is Some(_): use Patch::Merge (preserves other keys in .data)
      - api.patch(&self.name, &PatchParams::default(), &Patch::Merge(patch_secret)).await
      - If Err(kube::Error::Api(s)) if s.code == Some(404): fall through to create (step f)
      - Otherwise map errors as in get() and return
   f. If self.key is None OR after 404 from merge: use replace-or-create:
      - Try api.replace(): if Ok, return Ok(())
      - If Err 404: build full Secret with metadata (name, namespace, type_=Some("Opaque")), call api.create()
   g. Map all kube errors identically to get().
2. At module level: inventory::submit!(secretx_core::WritableBackendRegistration { name: "k8s", factory: |uri| K8sBackend::from_uri(uri).map(|b| Arc::new(b) as Arc<dyn WritableSecretStore>) })

Key design note: Patch::Merge on .data merges at the key level (RFC 7386) — only the patched key changes; other keys in the existing Secret are preserved. This is the correct semantic for ?key= targeted writes. For no-?key= writes, replace() overwrites the entire object so stale keys are removed.

Defensive programming rules:
- value.into_bytes() returns Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>; the ByteString clone is unavoidable but must be dropped immediately after the API call.
- Never log value bytes; log only namespace/name/key.
- The create fallback path must set type_=Some("Opaque") to produce a valid Opaque Secret.
- All error paths use specific variants.

Subagent instructions: Spawn research subagent to read k8s-openapi Secret struct source to confirm Patch::Merge serializes ByteString to base64 correctly. Spawn validation subagent to run cargo test -p secretx-k8s after implementation.

Tests (via W3B bead):
- put() on non-existent secret creates it with correct value
- put() on existing secret updates target key; other keys preserved (?key= path)
- put() without ?key= on existing multi-key secret: old keys gone, new single key present
- put() returns Err on 403 response
